articles.title: IMD issues Orange, Yellow warning for thunderstorm, heatwave in Odisha; rain likely In several districts - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued Orange and Yellow warnings for several districts of Odisha, forecasting thunderstorms with lightning, gusty winds and heatwave conditions over the next few days.
According to the IMD bulletin valid till March 15, light to moderate rain or thunderstorms are likely at one or two places in Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Balasore and Bhadrak, while the rest of the districts are expected to witness dry weather.
Under a Yellow Warning, thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds reaching 30–40 kmph are likely at isolated places in Mayurbhanj and Keonjhar.
The IMD has also warned of heatwave conditions in parts of Jharsuguda and Sambalpur districts.
Rainfall To Expand To More Districts
Between March 15 and March 16, rain or thunderstorms are likely in several districts including Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Dhenkanal, Nabarangpur, Malkangiri, Koraput, Rayagada, Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khordha, Nayagarh, Ganjam and Gajapati.
A Yellow Warning remains in effect for Mayurbhanj and Keonjhar for thunderstorms with lightning and gusty winds of 30–40 kmph. Heatwave conditions may also persist in Jharsuguda and Sambalpur.
Orange Warning For Thunderstorms On March 16–17
The IMD has issued an Orange Warning for Rayagada, Mayurbhanj and Keonjhar districts, where thunderstorms with lightning and gusty winds reaching 40–50 kmph are very likely at isolated places between March 16 and March 17.
A Yellow Warning has also been issued for many districts including Nabarangpur, Angul, Boudh, Kandhamal, Kalahandi, Dhenkanal, Malkangiri, Koraput, Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khordha, Nayagarh, Ganjam and Gajapati, where thunderstorms with wind speeds of 30–40 kmph may occur.
Thunderstorm Activity To Continue
For March 17–18, light to moderate rain or thunderstorms are likely in Mayurbhanj, Nabarangpur, Keonjhar, Malkangiri, Koraput, Rayagada, Ganjam and Gajapati, with a Yellow Warning issued for Mayurbhanj and Keonjhar.

From March 18–19, rainfall activity is expected mainly in Mayurbhanj and Keonjhar, while the rest of Odisha is likely to remain dry.
Similarly, between March 19–20, thunderstorms with light to moderate rain are likely in Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Rayagada, Ganjam and Gajapati, with dry weather expected elsewhere.
The IMD has advised people to remain alert during thunderstorms and avoid taking shelter under trees or near electric poles during lightning activity.
